title: Bundling the shorter command
question: How do people who install the tool get the /decide alias?
rationale: Ship the '/decide' shim inside the skill/ package so a clone installs both '/decision-tree' and '/decide' with one copy step. Chosen over README-only instructions because the alias then works out of the box with nothing to hand-create; the cost is one more small file to re-copy when it changes (the d42 sync trade).

tradeoffs.note: clone, run the install copy, and '/decide' works — no manual file creation from a snippet
the alias ships with the tool, not as separate copy-paste instructions a user might skip
one more file in skill/ to re-copy when the shim changes — the d42 copy trade
nothing extra in the package; just a few lines of docs
cloners must hand-create the shim from a heredoc — an easy step to skip, so many never get '/decide'

options.label: Don't ship the alias at all

tradeoffs.note: package stays minimal — one skill, nothing extra
cloners never get the easier command unless they invent it themselves
